sourceMovie.title: Dressed to Kill
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Dressed to Kill for the same reason it broke through in 1980: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a mystery rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Brian De Palma keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ghostland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dressed to Kill clicked for you, Ghostland (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Eyes Wide Shut
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dressed to Kill clicked for you, Eyes Wide Shut (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dressed to Kill?
faq.answer: Try Ghostland, Eyes Wide Shut, Basic Instinct—each shares DNA with Dressed to Kill while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dressed to Kill?
faq.answer: Dressed to Kill (1980) is best described as Thriller, Mystery, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dressed to Kill worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
